what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG ruler icon using currentColor strokes, with a tilted quadrilateral outline and five internal diagonal tick marks.
observations: The SVG has viewBox="0 0 24 24" and no explicit width or height.
The icon uses fill="none" and stroke="currentColor".
Stroke styling is set on the root element with width 1.5, round line caps, and round joins.
One path draws the tilted ruler body as a closed four-sided shape.
A second path draws five separate diagonal measurement marks.
standouts: The icon is color-inheriting through currentColor.
The ruler shape is rotated diagonally rather than drawn horizontally.
Measurement marks are grouped into a single compound path.
conventions: Uses a compact inline SVG structure.
Follows common outline-icon conventions with no fill, rounded strokes, and inherited color.
Uses path-only geometry rather than rect, line, or polygon elements.
concerns: Grouped tick marks in one path make individual mark styling or targeting unavailable within this file.
notes: A compact outline SVG for a diagonal ruler icon with inherited stroke color.
questions: Should the icon remain diagonal, or is orientation defined elsewhere by icon-set style?
Is stroke-width 1.5 a local choice or part of a broader icon convention?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a ruler icon, and the SVG content draws a ruler-like shape with measurement marks.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a lightweight, themeable ruler symbol for UI contexts where sizing, measurement, layout, or design tools are represented.
novelty: The ruler is represented as a diagonal closed polygon with tick marks embedded as short diagonal strokes.
contribution: Contributes a themeable measurement-tool icon using simple path geometry, rounded outline styling, and a compact SVG footprint suitable for reuse in a fused icon asset set.
summary_for_embedding: A compact 24x24 currentColor outline SVG icon depicting a diagonal ruler with five internal measurement tick marks.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for representing ruler, measurement, sizing, or design-tool concepts as a reusable UI icon.
